description: Los Choneros is an organized crime syndicate from Ecuador, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, robbery, and other crimes. Originating in Manabí Province in the 1990s, it functions as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ecuador. The group has significant influence over security officials and politicians, and its leader José Adolfo Macías Villamar escaped prison in 2024, triggering an armed conflict. As of 2025, the group remains active with an estimated 12,000-20,000 members.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Choneros gang’s relationships with mafia groups and cartels in Mexico, Colombia, and Albania, has resulted in Ecuador becoming one of the main sources of cocaine to Europe.
country_links.context: Los Choneros have relationships with mafia groups in Albania, resulting in Ecuador becoming a source of cocaine to Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: Los Choneros work with groups in Colombia, Peru, and Mexico, in order to traffic drugs like cocaine into the United States.
country_links.context: Los Choneros work with groups in Colombia to traffic drugs.
